text: A data communication system is made up of five components

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: 1. Message: the information (data) to be communicated – Consist of text, numbers, pictures, audio, or video

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: 2. Sender: the device that sends the data message

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: – Computer, workstation, telephone handset, video camera

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: 3. Receiver: the device that receives the message

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: – Computer, workstation, telephone handset, television, 4. Protocol: a set of rules that govern data communications 5. Medium: The physical path by which a message travels

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: twisted pair, coaxial cable, fiber-optic, radio waves

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: The basic building block of any communications facility is the transmission line

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: The business manager is concerned with a facility providing the required capacity, with acceptable reliability, at minimum cost

text: Host Roles Every computer on a network is called a host or end device. Servers are computers that provide information to end devices:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: email servers • web servers • file server

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Clients are computers that send requests to the servers to retrieve information:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: web page from a web server • email from an email server

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: It is possible to have a device be a client and a server in a Peer-to-Peer Network. This type of network design is only recommended for very small networks.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: An end device is where a message originates from or where it is received. Data originates with an end device, flows through the network, and arrives at an end device.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Intermediary Network Devices An intermediary device interconnects end devices. Examples include switches, wireless access points, routers, and firewalls.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Management of data as it flows through a network is also the role of an intermediary device, including:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Maintain information about what pathways exist in the network.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Notify other devices of errors and communication failures.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Physical topology diagrams illustrate the physical location of intermediary devices and cable installation.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Logical topology diagrams illustrate devices, ports, and the addressing scheme of the network.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Medium to Large Networks – many locations with hundreds or thousands of interconnected computers

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: World Wide Networks – connects hundreds of millions of computers world-wide – such as the internet

text: Size of the area covered • Number of users connected • Number and types of services available

text: Two most common types of networks:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Local Area Network (LAN) • Wide Area Network (WAN).

text: ➢middle between LAN and WAN ➢private or public network ➢high speed ➢large area

text: Data are sent out in a sequence of small chunks called packets

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Packets are passed from node to node along a path leading from source to destination

1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: The internet is a worldwide collection of interconnected LANs and WANs (Network of networks)

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: LANs are connected to each other using WANs.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: WANs may use copper wires, fiber optic cables, and wireless transmissions.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: The internet is not owned by any individual or group. The following groups were developed to help maintain structure on the internet:

1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: There are many ways to connect users and organizations to the internet:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Popular services for home users and small offices include broadband cable, broadband digital subscriber line (DSL), wireless WANs, and mobile services.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Organizations need faster connections to support IP phones, video conferencing and data center storage.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: A fault tolerant network limits the impact of a failure by limiting the number of affected devices. Multiple paths are required for fault tolerance.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: A scalable network can expand quickly and easily to support new users and applications without impacting the performance of services to existing users.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Voice and live video transmissions require higher expectations for those services being delivered. • Quality of Service (QoS) is the primary mechanism used to ensure

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: reliable delivery of content for all users.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: There are two main types of network security that must be addressed:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Network infrastructure security • Physical security of network devices

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Preventing unauthorized access to the devices

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Information Security • Protection of the information or data transmitted over the network

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: The role of the network must adjust and continually transform in order to be able to keep up with new technologies and end user devices as they constantly come to the market.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Several new networking trends that effect organizations and consumers:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) • Online collaboration • Video communications • Cloud computing

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) allows users to use their own devices giving them more opportunities and greater flexibility.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: BYOD allows end users to have the freedom to use personal tools to access information and communicate using their:

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: BYOD means any device, with any ownership, used anywhere.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Collaborate and work with others over the network on joint projects.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Collaboration is a very high priority for businesses and in education.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Collaboration tools including Cisco WebEx gives users a way to instantly connect and interact.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Video calls are made to anyone, regardless of where they are located.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Video conferencing is a powerful tool for communicating with others.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Video is becoming a critical requirement for effective collaboration.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Cisco TelePresence powers is one way of working where everyone,

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Cloud computing allows us to store personal files or backup our data on servers over the internet.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Applications can also be accessed using the Cloud.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Allows businesses to deliver to any device anywhere in the

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Cloud computing is made possible by data centers.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Smart home technology is a growing trend that allows technology to be integrated into every-day appliances which allows them to interconnect with other devices. Ovens might know what time to cook a meal for you by communicating with your calendar on what time you are scheduled to be home. Smart home technology is currently being developed for all rooms within a house.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Powerline Networking • Powerline networking can allow devices to connect to a LAN where data

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: network cables or wireless communications are not a viable option. • Using a standard powerline adapter, devices can connect to the LAN

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: wherever there is an electrical outlet by sending data on certain frequencies.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Powerline networking is especially useful when wireless access points cannot reach all the devices in the home.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Wireless Broadband In addition to DSL and cable, wireless is another option used to connect homes and small businesses to the internet.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Wireless Internet Service Provider (WISP) is an ISP that connects subscribers to designated access points or hotspots.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf

text: Wireless broadband is another solution for the home and small businesses.

metadata.filename: 1- Data Communications, Data Networks, and the Internet.pdf
